  <div
    class="search-input-container search-with-dialog position-relative d-flex flex-row flex-items-center tmp-mr-4 rounded"
    data-action="click:qbsearch-input#searchInputContainerClicked"
  >
      <button
        type="button"
        class="header-search-button placeholder  input-button form-control d-flex flex-1 flex-self-stretch flex-items-center no-wrap width-full py-0 pl-2 pr-0 text-left border-0 box-shadow-none"

        placeholder="Search or jump to..."

        data-analytics-event="{&quot;location&quot;:&quot;navbar&quot;,&quot;action&quot;:&quot;searchbar&quot;,&quot;context&quot;:&quot;global&quot;,&quot;tag&quot;:&quot;input&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;searchbar_input_global_navbar&quot;}"

</div>

    </div>
</modal-dialog></div>

  <div class="Overlay-headerContentWrap">

    </div>

      <button data-close-dialog-id="feedback-dialog" aria-label="Close" aria-label="Close" type="button" data-view-component="true" class="close-button Overlay-closeButton"><svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-x">
    <path d="M3.72 3.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0L8 6.94l3.22-3.22a.749.749 0 0 1 1.275.326.749.749 0 0 1-.215.734L9.06 8l3.22 3.22a.749.749 0 0 1-.326 1.275.749.749 0 0 1-.734-.215L8 9.06l-3.22 3.22a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042L6.94 8 3.72 4.78a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.06Z"></path>
</svg></button>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>

        <div data-view-component="true" class="Overlay-body">        <!-- '"` --><!-- </textarea></xmp> --></option></form><form id="code-search-feedback-form" data-turbo="false" action="/search/feedback" accept-charset="UTF-8" method="post"><input type="hidden" data-csrf="true" name="authenticity_token" value="tPE83o8b+hn4UIscynikFScxkZaevDdzEF4tCiOmQ/fRMPoKfeoODcYfhHwlYb13VWFe/TNOks72Z5NAL1kCfA==" />
          <p>We read every piece of feedback, and take your input very seriously.</p>
          <textarea name="feedback" class="form-control width-full mb-2" style="height: 120px" id="feedback"></textarea>
          <input name="include_email" id="include_email" aria-label="Include my email address so I can be contacted" class="form-control mr-2" type="checkbox">
          <label for="include_email" style="font-weight: normal">Include my email address so I can be contacted</label>
</form></div>
      </scrollable-region>
      <div data-view-component="true" class="Overlay-footer Overlay-footer--alignEnd">          <button data-close-dialog-id="feedback-dialog" type="button" data-view-component="true" class="btn">    Cancel
</button>

</button>
</div>
</dialog></dialog-helper>

<dialog-helper>

      <button data-close-dialog-id="custom-scopes-dialog" aria-label="Close" aria-label="Close" type="button" data-view-component="true" class="close-button Overlay-closeButton"><svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-x">
    <path d="M3.72 3.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0L8 6.94l3.22-3.22a.749.749 0 0 1 1.275.326.749.749 0 0 1-.215.734L9.06 8l3.22 3.22a.749.749 0 0 1-.326 1.275.749.749 0 0 1-.734-.215L8 9.06l-3.22 3.22a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042L6.94 8 3.72 4.78a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.06Z"></path>

  </div>

</div>
      <scrollable-region data-labelled-by="custom-scopes-dialog-title">
        <div data-view-component="true" class="Overlay-body">        <div data-target="custom-scopes.customScopesModalDialogFlash"></div>

        <div hidden class="create-custom-scope-form" data-target="custom-scopes.createCustomScopeForm">
        <!-- '"` --><!-- </textarea></xmp> --></option></form><form id="custom-scopes-dialog-form" data-turbo="false" action="/search/custom_scopes" accept-charset="UTF-8" method="post"><input type="hidden" data-csrf="true" name="authenticity_token" value="9yc/8C0FT8zKYe8Y2UJVvifo8iQCc7Iqe9r4uLpzBLWX1L45rXz5S4iGBipK5dh7o9MBdfJpvW112Shs+S+0Lw==" />
          <div data-target="custom-scopes.customScopesModalDialogFlash"></div>

          <input type="hidden" id="custom_scope_id" name="custom_scope_id" data-target="custom-scopes.customScopesIdField">

          <div class="form-group">
            <label for="custom_scope_name">Name</label>
            <auto-check src="/search/custom_scopes/check_name" required>
              <input
                type="text"
                name="custom_scope_name"
                id="custom_scope_name"
                data-target="custom-scopes.customScopesNameField"
                class="form-control"
                autocomplete="off"
                placeholder="github-ruby"
                required
                maxlength="50">
              <input type="hidden" data-csrf="true" value="cPm6wSr22n0At9SoluLeep8Frm3EeyX5N9mrx9nuxar3uSw2iu3Iok2lwBGyA1bmSUwRPG+g1M4s8C9o7SlgaA==" />
            </auto-check>
          </div>

          <div class="form-group">
            <label for="custom_scope_query">Query</label>
            <input
              type="text"
              name="custom_scope_query"
              id="custom_scope_query"
              data-target="custom-scopes.customScopesQueryField"
              class="form-control"
              autocomplete="off"
              placeholder="(repo:mona/a OR repo:mona/b) AND lang:python"
              required
              maxlength="500">
          </div>

          <p class="text-small color-fg-muted">
            To see all available qualifiers, see our <a class="Link--inTextBlock" href="https://docs.github.com/search-github/github-code-search/understanding-github-code-search-syntax">documentation</a>.
          </p>
</form>        </div>

        <div data-target="custom-scopes.manageCustomScopesForm">
          <div data-target="custom-scopes.list"></div>
        </div>

</div>
      </scrollable-region>
      <div data-view-component="true" class="Overlay-footer Overlay-footer--alignEnd Overlay-footer--divided">          <button data-action="click:custom-scopes#customScopesCancel" type="button" data-view-component="true" class="btn">    Cancel
</button>
          <button form="custom-scopes-dialog-form" data-action="click:custom-scopes#customScopesSubmit" data-target="custom-scopes.customScopesSubmitButton" type="submit" data-view-component="true" class="btn-primary btn">    Create saved search
</button>
</div>
</dialog></dialog-helper>

  </div>

</div>

2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSwagger™  is a project used to describe and document RESTful APIs.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe Swagger specification defines a set of files required to describe such an API. These files can then be used by the Swagger-UI project to display the API and Swagger-Codegen to generate clients in various languages. Additional utilities can also take advantage of the resulting files, such as testing tools.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe files describing the RESTful API in accordance with the Swagger specification are represented as JSON objects and conform to the JSON standards. YAML, being a superset of JSON, can be used as well to\nrepresent a Swagger specification file.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor example, if a field is said to have an array value, the JSON array representation will be used:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-js notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"{\n   \u0026quot;field\u0026quot; : [...]\n}\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e{\u003c/span\u003e\n   \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"field\"\u003c/span\u003e : \u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e[\u003c/span\u003e...\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e]\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e}\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhile the API is described using JSON it does not impose a JSON input/output to the API itself.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAll field names in the specification are \u003cstrong\u003ecase sensitive\u003c/strong\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe schema exposes two types of fields. Fixed fields, which have a declared name, and Patterned fields, which declare a regex pattern for the field name. Patterned fields can have multiple occurrences as long as each has a unique name.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFile Structure\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-file-structure\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: File Structure\" href=\"#file-structure\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe Swagger representation of the API is made of a single file. However, parts of the definitions can be split into separate files, at the discretion of the user. This is applicable for \u003ccode\u003e$ref\u003c/code\u003e fields in the specification as follows from the \u003ca href=\"http://json-schema.org\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eJSON Schema\u003c/a\u003e definitions.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eBy convention, the Swagger specification file is named \u003ccode\u003eswagger.json\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eData Types\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-data-types\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Data Types\" href=\"#data-types\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePrimitive data types in the Swagger Specification are based on the types supported by the \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-zyp-json-schema-04#section-3.5\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eJSON-Schema Draft 4\u003c/a\u003e. Models are described using the \u003ca href=\"#schema-object\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/a\u003e which is a subset of JSON Schema Draft 4.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAn additional primitive data type \u003ccode\u003e\"file\"\u003c/code\u003e is used by the \u003ca href=\"#parameter-object\"\u003eParameter Object\u003c/a\u003e and the \u003ca href=\"#response-object\"\u003eResponse Object\u003c/a\u003e to set the parameter type or the response as being a file.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-datatypeformat\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ePrimitives have an optional modifier property \u003ccode\u003eformat\u003c/code\u003e. Swagger uses several known formats to more finely define the data type being used. However, the \u003ccode\u003eformat\u003c/code\u003e property is an open \u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e-valued property, and can have any value to support documentation needs. Formats such as \u003ccode\u003e\"email\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"uuid\"\u003c/code\u003e, etc., can be used even though they are not defined by this specification. Types that are not accompanied by a \u003ccode\u003eformat\u003c/code\u003e property follow their definition from the JSON Schema (except for \u003ccode\u003efile\u003c/code\u003e type which is defined above). The formats defined by the Swagger Specification are:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eCommon Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003e\u003ca href=\"#dataTypeType\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003e\u003ca href=\"#dataTypeFormat\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eformat\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eComments\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003einteger\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003einteger\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eint32\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003esigned 32 bits\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003elong\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003einteger\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eint64\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003esigned 64 bits\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003efloat\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003enumber\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003efloat\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003edouble\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003enumber\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003edouble\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003estring\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ebyte\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebyte\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ebase64 encoded characters\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ebinary\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebinary\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eany sequence of octets\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eboolean\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003edate\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003edate\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAs defined by \u003ccode\u003efull-date\u003c/code\u003e - \u003ca href=\"https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3339#section-5.6\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eRFC3339\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003edateTime\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003edate-time\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAs defined by \u003ccode\u003edate-time\u003c/code\u003e - \u003ca href=\"https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3339#section-5.6\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eRFC3339\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003epassword\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003epassword\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eUsed to hint UIs the input needs to be obscured.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eSchema\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-schema\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Schema\" href=\"#schema\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eSwagger Object\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-swagger-object\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Swagger Object\" href=\"#swagger-object\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis is the root document object for the API specification. It combines what previously was the Resource Listing and API Declaration (version 1.2 and earlier) together into one document.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFixed Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-fixed-fields\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Fixed Fields\" href=\"#fixed-fields\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggerswagger\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eswagger\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e Specifies the Swagger Specification version being used. It can be used by the Swagger UI and other clients to interpret the API listing. The value MUST be \u003ccode\u003e\"2.0\"\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggerinfo\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003einfo\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#info-object\"\u003eInfo Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e Provides metadata about the API. The metadata can be used by the clients if needed.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggerhost\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ehost\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe host (name or ip) serving the API. This MUST be the host only and does not include the scheme nor sub-paths. It MAY include a port. If the \u003ccode\u003ehost\u003c/code\u003e is not included, the host serving the documentation is to be used (including the port). The \u003ccode\u003ehost\u003c/code\u003e does not support \u003ca href=\"#path-templating\"\u003epath templating\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggerbasepath\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ebasePath\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe base path on which the API is served, which is relative to the \u003ca href=\"#swaggerHost\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ehost\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e. If it is not included, the API is served directly under the \u003ccode\u003ehost\u003c/code\u003e. The value MUST start with a leading slash (\u003ccode\u003e/\u003c/code\u003e). The \u003ccode\u003ebasePath\u003c/code\u003e does not support \u003ca href=\"#path-templating\"\u003epath templating\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggerschemes\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eschemes\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe transfer protocol of the API. Values MUST be from the list: \u003ccode\u003e\"http\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"https\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"ws\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"wss\"\u003c/code\u003e. If the \u003ccode\u003eschemes\u003c/code\u003e is not included, the default scheme to be used is the one used to access the Swagger definition itself.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggerconsumes\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003econsumes\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA list of MIME types the APIs can consume. This is global to all APIs but can be overridden on specific API calls. Value MUST be as described under \u003ca href=\"#mime-types\"\u003eMime Types\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggerproduces\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eproduces\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA list of MIME types the APIs can produce. This is global to all APIs but can be overridden on specific API calls. Value MUST be as described under \u003ca href=\"#mime-types\"\u003eMime Types\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggerpaths\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003epaths\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#paths-object\"\u003ePaths Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The available paths and operations for the API.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggerdefinitions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edefinitions\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#definitions-object\"\u003eDefinitions Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAn object to hold data types produced and consumed by operations.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggerparameters\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eparameters\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#parameters-definitions-object\"\u003eParameters Definitions Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAn object to hold parameters that can be used across operations. This property \u003cem\u003edoes not\u003c/em\u003e define global parameters for all operations.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggerresponses\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eresponses\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#responses-definitions-object\"\u003eResponses Definitions Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAn object to hold responses that can be used across operations. This property \u003cem\u003edoes not\u003c/em\u003e define global responses for all operations.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggersecuritydefinitions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003esecurityDefinitions\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#security-definitions-object\"\u003eSecurity Definitions Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSecurity scheme definitions that can be used across the specification.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggersecurity\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003esecurity\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ca href=\"#security-requirement-object\"\u003eSecurity Requirement Object\u003c/a\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA declaration of which security schemes are applied for the API as a whole. The list of values describes alternative security schemes that can be used (that is, there is a logical OR between the security requirements). Individual operations can override this definition.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggertags\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etags\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ca href=\"#tag-object\"\u003eTag Object\u003c/a\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA list of tags used by the specification with additional metadata. The order of the tags can be used to reflect on their order by the parsing tools. Not all tags that are used by the \u003ca href=\"#operation-object\"\u003eOperation Object\u003c/a\u003e must be declared. The tags that are not declared may be organized randomly or based on the tools' logic. Each tag name in the list MUST be unique.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-swaggerexternaldocs\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexternalDocs\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#external-documentation-object\"\u003eExternal Documentation Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAdditional external documentation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 name=\"user-content-swaggerextensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x-\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with \u003ccode\u003ex-\u003c/code\u003e, for example, \u003ccode\u003ex-internal-id\u003c/code\u003e. The value can be \u003ccode\u003enull\u003c/code\u003e, a primitive, an array or an object. See \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eVendor Extensions\u003c/a\u003e for further details.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eInfo Object\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-info-object\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Info Object\" href=\"#info-object\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe object provides metadata about the API. name=\"user-content-infotitle\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etitle\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The title of the application.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-infodescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA short description of the application. \u003ca href=\"https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/#GitHub-flavored-markdown\"\u003eGFM syntax\u003c/a\u003e can be used for rich text representation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-infotermsofservice\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etermsOfService\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd name=\"user-content-infocontact\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003econtact\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#contact-object\"\u003eContact Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe contact information for the exposed API.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-infolicense\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003elicense\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#license-object\"\u003eLicense The path is appended to the \u003ca href=\"#swaggerBasePath\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebasePath\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e in order to construct the full URL.\nThe Paths may be empty, due to \u003ca href=\"#security-filtering\"\u003eACL constraints\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ePatterned Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-patterned-fields\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Patterned Fields\" href=\"#patterned-fields\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Pattern\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-pathspath\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e/{path}\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#path-item-object\"\u003ePath Item Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA relative path to an individual endpoint. The field name MUST begin with a slash. The path is appended to the \u003ca href=\"#swaggerBasePath\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebasePath\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e in order to construct the full URL. \u003ca href=\"#path-templating\"\u003ePath templating\u003c/a\u003e is allowed.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-pathsextensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x-\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with \u003ccode\u003ex-\u003c/code\u003e, for example, \u003ccode\u003ex-internal-id\u003c/code\u003e. The value can be \u003ccode\u003enull\u003c/code\u003e, a primitive, an array or an object. See \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eVendor Extensions\u003c/a\u003e for further details.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 The path itself is still exposed to the documentation viewer but they will not know which operations and parameters are available.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFixed Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-fixed-fields-4\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Fixed Fields\" href=\"#fixed-fields-4\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-pathitemref\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e$ref\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows for an external definition of this path item. The referenced structure MUST be in the format of a \u003ca href=\"#path-item-object\"\u003ePath Item Object\u003c/a\u003e. If there are conflicts between the referenced definition and this Path Item's definition, the behavior is \u003cem\u003eundefined\u003c/em\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-pathitemget\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eget\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#operation-object\"\u003eOperation Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA definition of a GET operation on this path.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-pathitemput\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eput\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#operation-object\"\u003eOperation Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA definition of a PUT operation on this path.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-pathitempost\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003epost\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#operation-object\"\u003eOperation Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA definition of a POST operation on this path.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-pathitemdelete\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edelete\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#operation-object\"\u003eOperation Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA definition of a DELETE operation on this path.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-pathitemoptions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eoptions\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#operation-object\"\u003eOperation Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA definition of a OPTIONS operation on this path.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-pathitemhead\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ehead\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#operation-object\"\u003eOperation Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA definition of a HEAD operation on this path.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-pathitempatch\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003epatch\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#operation-object\"\u003eOperation Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA definition of a PATCH operation on this path.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-pathitemparameters\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eparameters\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ca href=\"#parameter-object\"\u003eParameter Object\u003c/a\u003e | \u003ca href=\"#reference-object\"\u003eReference Object\u003c/a\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA list of parameters that are applicable for all the operations described under this path. These parameters can be overridden at the operation level, but cannot be removed there. The list MUST NOT include duplicated parameters. A unique parameter is defined by a combination of a \u003ca href=\"#parameterName\"\u003ename\u003c/a\u003e and \u003ca href=\"#parameterIn\"\u003elocation\u003c/a\u003e. The list can use the \u003ca href=\"#reference-object\"\u003eReference Object\u003c/a\u003e to link to parameters that are defined at the \u003ca href=\"#swaggerParameters\"\u003eSwagger Object's parameters\u003c/a\u003e. There can be one \"body\" parameter at most.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ePatterned Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-patterned-fields-1\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Patterned Fields\" href=\"#patterned-fields-1\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Pattern\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-pathitemextensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x-\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The value can be \u003ccode\u003enull\u003c/code\u003e, a primitive, an array or an object. Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-operationtags\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etags\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA list of tags for API documentation control. Tags can be used for logical grouping of operations by resources or any other qualifier.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-operationsummary\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003esummary\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA short summary of what the operation does. For maximum readability in the swagger-ui, this field SHOULD be less than 120 characters.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-operationdescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA verbose explanation of the operation behavior. \u003ca href=\"https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/#GitHub-flavored-markdown\"\u003eGFM syntax\u003c/a\u003e can be used for rich text representation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-operationexternaldocs\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexternalDocs\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#external-documentation-object\"\u003eExternal Documentation Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAdditional external documentation for this operation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-operationid\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eoperationId\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eUnique string used to identify the operation. The id MUST be unique among all operations described in the API. Tools and libraries MAY use the operationId to uniquely identify an operation, therefore, it is recommended to follow common programming naming conventions.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-operationconsumes\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003econsumes\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA list of MIME types the operation can consume. This overrides the \u003ca href=\"#swaggerConsumes\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003econsumes\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e definition at the Swagger Object. An empty value MAY be used to clear the global definition. Value MUST be as described under \u003ca href=\"#mime-types\"\u003eMime Types\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-operationproduces\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eproduces\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA list of MIME types the operation can produce. This overrides the \u003ca href=\"#swaggerProduces\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eproduces\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e definition at the Swagger Object. An empty value MAY be used to clear the global definition. Value MUST be as described under \u003ca href=\"#mime-types\"\u003eMime Types\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-operationparameters\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eparameters\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ca href=\"#parameter-object\"\u003eParameter Object\u003c/a\u003e | \u003ca href=\"#reference-object\"\u003eReference Object\u003c/a\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA list of parameters that are applicable for this operation. If a parameter is already defined at the \u003ca href=\"#pathItemParameters\"\u003ePath Item\u003c/a\u003e, the new definition will override it, but can never remove it. The list MUST NOT include duplicated parameters. A unique parameter is defined by a combination of a \u003ca href=\"#parameterName\"\u003ename\u003c/a\u003e and \u003ca href=\"#parameterIn\"\u003elocation\u003c/a\u003e. The list can use the \u003ca href=\"#reference-object\"\u003eReference Object\u003c/a\u003e to link to parameters that are defined at the \u003ca href=\"#swaggerParameters\"\u003eSwagger Object's parameters\u003c/a\u003e. There can be one \"body\" parameter at most.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-operationresponses\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eresponses\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#responses-object\"\u003eResponses Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The list of possible responses as they are returned from executing this operation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-operationschemes\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eschemes\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe transfer protocol for the operation. Values MUST be from the list: \u003ccode\u003e\"http\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"https\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"ws\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"wss\"\u003c/code\u003e. The value overrides the Swagger Object \u003ca href=\"#swaggerSchemes\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eschemes\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e definition.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-operationdeprecated\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edeprecated\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDeclares this operation to be deprecated. Usage of the declared operation should be refrained. Default value is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-operationsecurity\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003esecurity\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ca href=\"#security-requirement-object\"\u003eSecurity Requirement Object\u003c/a\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA declaration of which security schemes are applied for this operation. The list of values describes alternative security schemes that can be used (that is, there is a logical OR between the security requirements). This definition overrides any declared top-level \u003ca href=\"#swaggerSecurity\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003esecurity\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e. To remove a top-level security declaration, an empty array can be used.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ePatterned Objects\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-patterned-objects-4\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Patterned Objects\" href=\"#patterned-objects-4\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Pattern\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-operationextensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x-\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with \u003ccode\u003ex-\u003c/code\u003e, for example, \u003ccode\u003ex-internal-id\u003c/code\u003e. The value can be \u003ccode\u003enull\u003c/code\u003e, a primitive, an array or an object. See \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eVendor Extensions\u003c/a\u003e for further details.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"{\n  \u0026quot;tags\u0026quot;: [\n    \u0026quot;pet\u0026quot;\n  ],\n  \u0026quot;summary\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;Updates a pet in the store with form data\u0026quot;,\n  \u0026quot;description\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;\u0026quot;,\n  \u0026quot;operationId\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;updatePetWithForm\u0026quot;,\n  \u0026quot;consumes\u0026quot;: [\n    \u0026quot;application/x-www-form-urlencoded\u0026quot;\n  ],\n  \u0026quot;produces\u0026quot;: [\n    \u0026quot;application/json\u0026quot;,\n    \u0026quot;application/xml\u0026quot;\n  ],\n  \u0026quot;parameters\u0026quot;: [\n    {\n      \u0026quot;name\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;petId\u0026quot;,\n      \u0026quot;in\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;path\u0026quot;,\n      \u0026quot;description\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;ID of pet that needs to be updated\u0026quot;,\n      \u0026quot;required\u0026quot;: true,\n      \u0026quot;type\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;string\u0026quot;\n    },\n    {\n      \u0026quot;name\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;name\u0026quot;,\n      \u0026quot;in\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;formData\u0026quot;,\n      \u0026quot;description\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;Updated name of the pet\u0026quot;,\n      \u0026quot;required\u0026quot;: false,\n      \u0026quot;type\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;string\u0026quot;\n status of the pet\u0026quot;,\n      \u0026quot;required\u0026quot;: false,\n      \u0026quot;type\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;string\u0026quot;\n    }\n  ],\n  \u0026quot;responses\u0026quot;: {\n    \u0026quot;200\u0026quot;: {\n      \u0026quot;description\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;Pet updated.\u0026quot;\n    },\n    \u0026quot;405\u0026quot;: {\n      \u0026quot;description\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;Invalid input\u0026quot;\n    }\n  },\n  \u0026quot;security\u0026quot;: [\n    {\n      \u0026quot;petstore_auth\u0026quot;: [\n        \u0026quot;write:pets\u0026quot;,\n        \u0026quot;read:pets\u0026quot;\n      ]\n    }\n  ]\n}\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e{\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"tags\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e[\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"pet\"\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e]\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"summary\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"Updates a pet in the store with form data\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"description\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"operationId\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"updatePetWithForm\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"consumes\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e[\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\"\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e]\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"produces\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e[\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"application/json\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"application/xml\"\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e]\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"parameters\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e[\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e{\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"name\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"petId\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"in\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"path\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"description\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"ID of pet that needs to be updated\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"required\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003etrue\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"type\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"string\"\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e}\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e{\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"name\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"name\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"in\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"formData\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"description\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"Updated name of the pet\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"required\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003efalse\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"type\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"string\"\u003c/span\u003e\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"description\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"Updated status of the pet\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e,\u003c/span\u003e\n      \u003cspan data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDescribes a single operation parameter.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA unique parameter is defined by a combination of a \u003ca href=\"#parameterName\"\u003ename\u003c/a\u003e and \u003ca href=\"#parameterIn\"\u003elocation\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThere are five possible parameter types.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003ePath - Used together with \u003ca href=\"#path-templating\"\u003ePath Templating\u003c/a\u003e, where the parameter value is actually part of the operation's URL. This does not include the host or base path of the API. For example, in \u003ccode\u003e/items/{itemId}\u003c/code\u003e, the path parameter is \u003ccode\u003eitemId\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eQuery - Parameters that are appended to the URL. For example, in \u003ccode\u003e/items?id=###\u003c/code\u003e, the query parameter is \u003ccode\u003eid\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eHeader - Custom headers that are expected as part of the request.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eBody - The payload that's appended to the HTTP request. Since there can only be one payload, there can only be \u003cem\u003eone\u003c/em\u003e body parameter. The name of the body parameter has no effect on the parameter itself and is used for documentation purposes only. Since Form parameters are also in the payload, body and form parameters cannot exist together for the same operation.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eForm - Used to describe the payload of an HTTP request when either \u003ccode\u003eapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003emultipart/form-data\u003c/code\u003e or both are used as the content type of the request (in Swagger's definition, the \u003ca href=\"#operationConsumes\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003econsumes\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e property of an operation). This is the only parameter type that can be used to send files, thus supporting the \u003ccode\u003efile\u003c/code\u003e type. Since form parameters are sent in the payload, they cannot be declared together with a body parameter for the same operation. Form parameters have a different format based on the content-type used (for further details, consult \u003ca href=\"http://www.w3.org/TR/html401/interact/forms.html#h-17.13.4\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttp://www.w3.org/TR/html401/interact/forms.html#h-17.13.4\u003c/a\u003e):\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded\u003c/code\u003e - Similar to the format of Query parameters but as a payload. For example, \u003ccode\u003efoo=1\u0026amp;bar=swagger\u003c/code\u003e - both \u003ccode\u003efoo\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003ebar\u003c/code\u003e are form parameters. This is normally used for simple parameters that are being transferred.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003emultipart/form-data\u003c/code\u003e - each parameter takes a section in the payload with an internal header. For example, for the header \u003ccode\u003eContent-Disposition: form-data; name=\"submit-name\"\u003c/code\u003e the name of the parameter is \u003ccode\u003esubmit-name\u003c/code\u003e. This type of form parameters is more commonly used for file transfers.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFixed Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-fixed-fields-7\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Fixed Fields\" href=\"#fixed-fields-7\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parametername\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ename\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The name of the parameter. Parameter names are \u003cem\u003ecase sensitive\u003c/em\u003e. \u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cli\u003eIf \u003ca href=\"#parameterIn\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ein\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e is \u003ccode\u003e\"path\"\u003c/code\u003e, the \u003ccode\u003ename\u003c/code\u003e field MUST correspond to the associated path segment from the \u003ca href=\"#pathsPath\"\u003epath\u003c/a\u003e field in the \u003ca href=\"#paths-object\"\u003ePaths Object\u003c/a\u003e. See \u003ca href=\"#path-templating\"\u003ePath Templating\u003c/a\u003e for further information.\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003eFor all other cases, the \u003ccode\u003ename\u003c/code\u003e corresponds to the parameter name used based on the \u003ca href=\"#parameterIn\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ein\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e property.\u003c/li\u003e\u003c/ul\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parameterin\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ein\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The location of the parameter. Possible values are \"query\", \"header\", \"path\", \"formData\" or \"body\".\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parameterdescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA brief description of the parameter. This could contain examples of use.  \u003ca href=\"https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/#GitHub-flavored-markdown\"\u003eGFM syntax\u003c/a\u003e can be used for rich text representation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parameterrequired\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003erequired\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDetermines whether this parameter is mandatory. If the parameter is \u003ca href=\"#parameterIn\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ein\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e \"path\", this property is \u003cstrong\u003erequired\u003c/strong\u003e and its value MUST be \u003ccode\u003etrue\u003c/code\u003e. Otherwise, the property MAY be included and its default value is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf \u003ca href=\"#parameterIn\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ein\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e is \u003ccode\u003e\"body\"\u003c/code\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parameterschema\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eschema\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#schema-object\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The schema defining the type used for the body parameter.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf \u003ca href=\"#parameterIn\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ein\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e is any value other than \u003ccode\u003e\"body\"\u003c/code\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parametertype\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etype\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The type of the parameter. Since the parameter is not located at the request body, it is limited to simple types (that is, not an object). The value MUST be one of \u003ccode\u003e\"string\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"number\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"integer\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"boolean\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"array\"\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003e\"file\"\u003c/code\u003e. If \u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e is \u003ccode\u003e\"file\"\u003c/code\u003e, the \u003ca href=\"#operationConsumes\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003econsumes\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e MUST be either \u003ccode\u003e\"multipart/form-data\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\" application/x-www-form-urlencoded\"\u003c/code\u003e or both and the parameter MUST be \u003ca href=\"#parameterIn\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ein\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e \u003ccode\u003e\"formData\"\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parameterformat\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eformat\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe extending format for the previously mentioned \u003ca href=\"#parameterType\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e. See \u003ca href=\"#dataTypeFormat\"\u003eData Type Formats\u003c/a\u003e for further details.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parameterallowemptyvalue\"\u003eallowEmptyValue\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters. This is valid only for either \u003ccode\u003equery\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003eformData\u003c/code\u003e parameters and allows you to send a parameter with a name only or  an empty value. Default value is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parameteritems\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eitems\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#items-object\"\u003eItems Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired if \u003ca href=\"#parameterType\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e is \"array\".\u003c/strong\u003e Describes the type of items in the array.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parametercollectionformat\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ecollectionFormat\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDetermines the format of the array if type array is used. Possible values are: \u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecsv\u003c/code\u003e - comma separated values \u003ccode\u003efoo,bar\u003c/code\u003e. \u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003essv\u003c/code\u003e - space separated values \u003ccode\u003efoo bar\u003c/code\u003e. \u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003etsv\u003c/code\u003e - tab separated values \u003ccode\u003efoo\\tbar\u003c/code\u003e. \u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003epipes\u003c/code\u003e - pipe separated values \u003ccode\u003efoo|bar\u003c/code\u003e. \u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003emulti\u003c/code\u003e - corresponds to multiple parameter instances instead of multiple values for a single instance \u003ccode\u003efoo=bar\u0026amp;foo=baz\u003c/code\u003e. This is valid only for parameters \u003ca href=\"#parameterIn\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ein\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e \"query\" or \"formData\". \u003c/li\u003e\u003c/ul\u003e Default value is \u003ccode\u003ecsv\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parameterdefault\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edefault\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e*\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDeclares the value of the parameter that the server will use if none is provided, for example a \"count\" to control the number of results per page might default to 100 if not supplied by the client in the request. (Note: \"default\" has no meaning for required parameters.)  See \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-6.2\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttps://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-6.2\u003c/a\u003e. Unlike JSON Schema this value MUST conform to the defined \u003ca href=\"#parameterType\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e for this parameter.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parametermaximum\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003emaximum\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003enumber\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSee \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.2\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttps://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.2\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parameterexclusivemaximum\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexclusiveMaximum\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSee \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.2\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttps://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.2\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parameterminimum\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eminimum\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003enumber\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSee \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.3\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttps://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.3\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parameterexclusiveminimum\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexclusiveMinimum\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSee \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.3\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttps://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.3\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parametermaxlength\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003emaxLength\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003einteger\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSee \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.2.1\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttps://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.2.1\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parameterminlength\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eminLength\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003einteger\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSee \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.2.2\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttps://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.2.2\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-parameterpattern\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003epattern\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSee \u003ca The value MUST be one of \u003ccode\u003e\"string\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"number\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"integer\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"boolean\"\u003c/code\u003e, or \u003ccode\u003e\"array\"\u003c/code\u003e. Files and models are not allowed.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-itemsformat\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eformat\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe extending format for the previously mentioned \u003ca href=\"#parameterType\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e. See \u003ca href=\"#dataTypeFormat\"\u003eData Type Formats\u003c/a\u003e for further details.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-itemsitems\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eitems\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#items-object\"\u003eItems Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired if \u003ca href=\"#itemsType\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e is \"array\".\u003c/strong\u003e Describes the type of items in the array.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-itemscollectionformat\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ecollectionFormat\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDetermines the format of the array if type array is used. Possible values are: \u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecsv\u003c/code\u003e - comma separated values \u003ccode\u003efoo,bar\u003c/code\u003e. \u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003essv\u003c/code\u003e - space separated values \u003ccode\u003efoo bar\u003c/code\u003e. \u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003etsv\u003c/code\u003e - tab separated values \u003ccode\u003efoo\\tbar\u003c/code\u003e. \u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003epipes\u003c/code\u003e - pipe separated values \u003ccode\u003efoo|bar\u003c/code\u003e. \u003c/li\u003e\u003c/ul\u003e Default value is \u003ccode\u003ecsv\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-itemsdefault\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edefault\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e*\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDeclares the value of the item that the server will use if none is provided. (Note: \"default\" has no meaning for required items.) See \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-6.2\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttps://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-6.2\u003c/a\u003e. Unlike JSON Schema this value MUST conform to the defined \u003ca href=\"#itemsType\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e for the data type.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-itemsmaximum\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003emaximum\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd name=\"user-content-itemsextensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x-\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with \u003ccode\u003ex-\u003c/code\u003e, for example, \u003ccode\u003ex-internal-id\u003c/code\u003e. The value can be \u003ccode\u003enull\u003c/code\u003e, a primitive, an array or an object. See \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eVendor Extensions\u003c/a\u003e for further class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"minLength\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e2\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e}\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e2\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAn array of arrays, the internal array being of type integer, numbers must be between 0 and 63 class=\"pl-ent\"\u003etype\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003earray\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eitems\u003c/span\u003e:\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003etype\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003einteger\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003eminimum\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eResponses Object\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-responses-object\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Responses Object\" href=\"#responses-object\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA container for the expected responses of an operation. The container maps a HTTP response code to the expected response. It is not expected from the documentation to necessarily cover all possible HTTP response codes, since they may not be known in advance. However, it is expected from the documentation to cover a successful operation response and any known errors.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe \u003ccode\u003edefault\u003c/code\u003e can be used as the default response object for all HTTP codes that are not covered individually by the specification.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe \u003ccode\u003eResponses Object\u003c/code\u003e MUST contain at least one response code, and it SHOULD be the response for a successful operation call.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFixed Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-fixed-fields-9\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink:
