name=\"user-content-responsesdefault\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edefault\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#response-object\"\u003eResponse Object\u003c/a\u003e | \u003ca href=\"#reference-object\"\u003eReference Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe documentation of responses other than the ones declared for specific HTTP response codes. It can be used to cover undeclared responses. \u003ca href=\"#reference-object\"\u003eReference Object\u003c/a\u003e can be used to link to a response that is defined at the \u003ca href=\"#swaggerResponses\"\u003eSwagger Object's responses\u003c/a\u003e section.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-responsescode\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e{\u003ca href=\"#http-status-codes\"\u003eHTTP Status Code\u003c/a\u003e}\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#response-object\"\u003eResponse Object\u003c/a\u003e | \u003ca href=\"#reference-object\"\u003eReference Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAny \u003ca href=\"#http-status-codes\"\u003eHTTP status code\u003c/a\u003e can be used as the property name (one property per HTTP status code). Describes the expected response for that HTTP status code.  \u003ca href=\"#reference-object\"\u003eReference Object\u003c/a\u003e can be used to link to a response that is defined at the \u003ca href=\"#swaggerResponses\"\u003eSwagger Object's responses\u003c/a\u003e section.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-responsesextensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x-\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with \u003ccode\u003ex-\u003c/code\u003e, for example, \u003ccode\u003ex-internal-id\u003c/code\u003e. The value can be \u003ccode\u003enull\u003c/code\u003e, a primitive, an array or an object. See \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eVendor Extensions\u003c/a\u003e for further details.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 representation.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-responseschema\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eschema\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#schema-object\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA definition of the response structure. It can be a primitive, an array or an object. If this field does not exist, it means no content is returned as part of the response. As an extension to the \u003ca href=\"#schema-object\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/a\u003e, its root \u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e value may also be \u003ccode\u003e\"file\"\u003c/code\u003e. This SHOULD be accompanied by a relevant \u003ccode\u003eproduces\u003c/code\u003e mime-type.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-headerdescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA short description of the header.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-headertype\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etype\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The type of the object. The value MUST be one of \u003ccode\u003e\"string\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"number\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"integer\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"boolean\"\u003c/code\u003e, or \u003ccode\u003e\"array\"\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-headerformat\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eformat\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe extending format for the previously mentioned \u003ca href=\"#stType\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e. See \u003ca href=\"#dataTypeFormat\"\u003eData Type Formats\u003c/a\u003e for further details.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-headeritems\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eitems\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#items-object\"\u003eItems Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired if \u003ca href=\"#stType\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e is \"array\".\u003c/strong\u003e Describes the type of items in the array.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-headercollectionformat\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ecollectionFormat\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDetermines the format of the array if type array is used. Possible values are: \u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecsv\u003c/code\u003e - comma separated values \u003ccode\u003efoo,bar\u003c/code\u003e. \u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003essv\u003c/code\u003e - space separated values \u003ccode\u003efoo bar\u003c/code\u003e. \u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003etsv\u003c/code\u003e - tab separated values \u003ccode\u003efoo\\tbar\u003c/code\u003e. \u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003epipes\u003c/code\u003e - pipe separated values \u003ccode\u003efoo|bar\u003c/code\u003e. \u003c/li\u003e\u003c/ul\u003e Default value is \u003ccode\u003ecsv\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-headerdefault\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edefault\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e*\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDeclares the value of the header that the server will use if none is provided. (Note: \"default\" has no meaning for required headers.) See \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-6.2\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehttps://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-6.2\u003c/a\u003e. Unlike JSON Schema this value MUST conform to the defined \u003ca href=\"#headerDefault\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e for the header.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-headermaximum\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003emaximum\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003enumber\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSee \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.2\" name=\"user-content-headermaximum\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexclusiveMaximum\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSee align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-headerextensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x-\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with \u003ccode\u003ex-\u003c/code\u003e, for example, \u003ccode\u003ex-internal-id\u003c/code\u003e. The value can be \u003ccode\u003enull\u003c/code\u003e, a primitive, an array or an object. See \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eVendor Extensions\u003c/a\u003e for further details.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 referencing other definitions in the specification. It can be used to reference parameters and responses that are defined at the top level for reuse.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe Reference Object is a \u003ca href=\"http://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-pbryan-zyp-json-ref-02\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eJSON Reference\u003c/a\u003e that uses a \u003ca href=\"http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6901\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eJSON Pointer\u003c/a\u003e as its value. For this specification, only \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-zyp-json-schema-04#section-7.2.3\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ecanonical dereferencing\u003c/a\u003e is supported.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFixed Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"{\n  \u0026quot;$ref\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;definitions.json#/Pet\u0026quot;\n}\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e{\u003c/span\u003e\n  \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"$ref\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"definitions.json#/Pet\"\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"$ref: 'definitions.yaml#/Pet'\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e$ref\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003edefinitions.yaml#/Pet\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch4 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/h4\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-schema-object\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Schema Object\" href=\"#schema-object\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe Schema Object allows the definition of input and output data types. These types can be objects, but also primitives and arrays. This object is based on the \u003ca href=\"http://json-schema.org/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eJSON Schema Specification Draft 4\u003c/a\u003e and uses a predefined subset of it. On top of this subset, there are extensions provided by this specification to allow for more complete documentation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFurther information about the properties can be found in \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-zyp-json-schema-04\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eJSON Schema Core\u003c/a\u003e and \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eJSON Schema Validation\u003c/a\u003e. Unless stated otherwise, the property definitions follow the JSON Schema specification as referenced here.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe following properties are taken directly from the JSON Schema definition and follow the same specifications:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e$ref - As a \u003ca href=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-pbryan-zyp-json-ref-03\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eJSON Reference\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eformat (See \u003ca href=\"#dataTypeFormat\"\u003eData Type Formats\u003c/a\u003e for further details)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003etitle\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003edescription (\u003ca href=\"https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/#GitHub-flavored-markdown\"\u003eGFM syntax\u003c/a\u003e can be used for rich text representation)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003edefault (Unlike JSON Schema, the value MUST conform to the defined type for the Schema Object)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003emultipleOf\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003emaximum\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eexclusiveMaximum\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eminimum\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eexclusiveMinimum\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003emaxLength\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eminLength\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003epattern\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003emaxItems\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eminItems\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003euniqueItems\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003emaxProperties\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eminProperties\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003erequired\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eenum\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003etype\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe following properties are taken from the JSON Schema definition but their definitions were adjusted to the Swagger Specification. Their definition is the same as the one from JSON Schema, only where the original definition references the JSON Schema definition, the \u003ca href=\"#schema-object\"\u003eSchema Object\u003c/a\u003e definition is used instead.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eitems\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eallOf\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eproperties\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eadditionalProperties\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOther than the JSON Schema subset fields, the following fields may be used for further schema documentation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFixed Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-fixed-fields-13\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Fixed Fields\" href=\"#fixed-fields-13\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemadiscriminator\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ediscriminator\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAdds support for polymorphism. The discriminator is the schema property name that is used to differentiate between other schema that inherit this schema. The property name used MUST be defined at this schema and it MUST be in the \u003ccode\u003erequired\u003c/code\u003e property list. When used, the value MUST be the name of this schema or any schema that inherits it.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemareadonly\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ereadOnly\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRelevant only for Schema \u003ccode\u003e\"properties\"\u003c/code\u003e definitions. Declares the property as \"read only\". This means that it MAY be sent as part of a response but MUST NOT be sent as part of the request. Properties marked as \u003ccode\u003ereadOnly\u003c/code\u003e being \u003ccode\u003etrue\u003c/code\u003e SHOULD NOT be in the \u003ccode\u003erequired\u003c/code\u003e list of the defined schema. Default value is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemaxml\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003exml\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#xml-object\"\u003eXML Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThis MAY be used only on properties schemas. It has no effect on root schemas. Adds Additional metadata to describe the XML representation format of this property.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemaexternaldocs\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexternalDocs\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#external-documentation-object\"\u003eExternal Documentation Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAdditional external documentation for this schema.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemaexample\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexample\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA free-form property to include an example of an instance for this schema.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ePatterned Objects\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-patterned-objects-9\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Patterned Objects\" href=\"#patterned-objects-9\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Pattern\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-schemaextensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x-\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with \u003ccode\u003ex-\u003c/code\u003e, for example, \u003ccode\u003ex-internal-id\u003c/code\u003e. The value can be \u003ccode\u003enull\u003c/code\u003e, a primitive, an array or an object. See \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eVendor Extensions\u003c/a\u003e for further details.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch6 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eComposition and Inheritance (Polymorphism)\u003c/h6\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-composition-and-inheritance-polymorphism\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Composition and Inheritance (Polymorphism)\" href=\"#composition-and-inheritance-polymorphism\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSwagger allows combining and extending model definitions using the \u003ccode\u003eallOf\u003c/code\u003e property of JSON Schema, in effect offering model composition. \u003ccode\u003eallOf\u003c/code\u003e takes in an array of object definitions that are validated \u003cem\u003eindependently\u003c/em\u003e but together compose a single object.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhile composition offers model extensibility, it does not imply a hierarchy between the models. To support polymorphism, Swagger adds the support of the \u003ccode\u003ediscriminator\u003c/code\u003e field. When used, the \u003ccode\u003ediscriminator\u003c/code\u003e will be the name of the property used to decide which schema definition is used to validate the structure of the model. As such, the \u003ccode\u003ediscriminator\u003c/code\u003e field MUST be a required field. The value of the chosen property has to be the friendly name given to the model under the \u003ccode\u003edefinitions\u003c/code\u003e property. As such, inline schema definitions, which do not have a given id, \u003cem\u003ecannot\u003c/em\u003e be used in polymorphism.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch6 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eXML Modeling\u003c/h6\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-xml-modeling\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: XML Modeling\" href=\"#xml-modeling\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe \u003ca href=\"#schemaXml\"\u003exml\u003c/a\u003e property allows extra definitions when translating the JSON definition to XML. The \u003ca href=\"#xml-object\"\u003eXML Object\u003c/a\u003e contains additional information about the available options.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eSchema Object Examples\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-schema-object-examples\" class=\"anchor\" When defined within the Items Object (\u003ccode\u003eitems\u003c/code\u003e), it will affect the name of the individual XML elements within the list. When defined alongside \u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e being \u003ccode\u003earray\u003c/code\u003e (outside the \u003ccode\u003eitems\u003c/code\u003e), it will affect the wrapping element and only if \u003ccode\u003ewrapped\u003c/code\u003e is \u003ccode\u003etrue\u003c/code\u003e. If \u003ccode\u003ewrapped\u003c/code\u003e is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e, it will be ignored.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-xmlnamespace\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003enamespace\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe URL of the namespace definition. Value SHOULD be in the form of a URL.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-xmlprefix\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eprefix\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe prefix to be used for the \u003ca href=\"#xmlName\"\u003ename\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-xmlattribute\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eattribute\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDeclares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element. Default value is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-xmlwrapped\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ewrapped\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eboolean\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eMAY be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped (for example, \u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;books\u0026gt;\u0026lt;book/\u0026gt;\u0026lt;book/\u0026gt;\u0026lt;/books\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e) or unwrapped (\u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;book/\u0026gt;\u0026lt;book/\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e). Default value is \u003ccode\u003efalse\u003c/code\u003e. The definition takes effect only when defined alongside \u003ccode\u003etype\u003c/code\u003e being \u003ccode\u003earray\u003c/code\u003e (outside the \u003ccode\u003eitems\u003c/code\u003e).\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ePatterned Objects\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-patterned-objects-10\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Patterned Objects\" href=\"#patterned-objects-10\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Pattern\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-xmlextensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x-\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with \u003ccode\u003ex-\u003c/code\u003e, for example, \u003ccode\u003ex-internal-id\u003c/code\u003e. The value can be \u003ccode\u003enull\u003c/code\u003e, a primitive, an array or an object. definition of a security scheme that can be used by the operations. Supported schemes are basic authentication, an API key (either as a header or as a query parameter) and OAuth2's common flows (implicit, password, application and access code).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFixed Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-fixed-fields-15\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Fixed Fields\" href=\"#fixed-fields-15\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eField Name\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eValidity\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemetype\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etype\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The type of the security scheme. Valid values are \u003ccode\u003e\"basic\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"apiKey\"\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003e\"oauth2\"\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemedescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eA short description for security scheme.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemename\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ename\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eapiKey\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The name of the header or query parameter to be used.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemein\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ein\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eapiKey\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired\u003c/strong\u003e The location of the API key. Valid values are \u003ccode\u003e\"query\"\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003e\"header\"\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemeflow\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eflow\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eoauth2\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The flow used by the OAuth2 security scheme. Valid values are \u003ccode\u003e\"implicit\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"password\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"application\"\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003e\"accessCode\"\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemeauthorizationurl\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eauthorizationUrl\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eoauth2\u003c/code\u003e (\u003ccode\u003e\"implicit\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"accessCode\"\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The authorization URL to be used for this flow. This SHOULD be in the form of a URL.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemetokenurl\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etokenUrl\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eoauth2\u003c/code\u003e (\u003ccode\u003e\"password\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"application\"\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e\"accessCode\"\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The token URL to be used for this flow. This SHOULD be in the form of a URL.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityschemescopes\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003escopes\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"#scopes-object\"\u003eScopes Object\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eoauth2\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequired.\u003c/strong\u003e The available scopes for the OAuth2 security scheme.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 name=\"user-content-securityschemeextensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x-\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with \u003ccode\u003ex-\u003c/code\u003e, for example, \u003ccode\u003ex-internal-id\u003c/code\u003e. The value can be \u003ccode\u003enull\u003c/code\u003e, a primitive, an array or an object. See \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eVendor Extensions\u003c/a\u003e for further name=\"user-content-scopesextensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x-\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003eAny\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAllows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with \u003ccode\u003ex-\u003c/code\u003e, for example, \u003ccode\u003ex-internal-id\u003c/code\u003e. The value can be \u003ccode\u003enull\u003c/code\u003e, a primitive, an array or an object. See \u003ca href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003eVendor Extensions\u003c/a\u003e for further The object can have multiple security schemes declared in it which are all required (that is, there is a logical AND between the schemes).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe name used for each property MUST correspond to a security scheme declared in the \u003ca href=\"#security-definitions-object\"\u003eSecurity Definitions\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch5 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ePatterned Fields\u003c/h5\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-patterned-fields-13\" Pattern\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth align=\"center\"\u003eType\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDescription\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ca name=\"user-content-securityrequirementsname\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e{name}\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e[\u003ccode\u003estring\u003c/code\u003e]\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eEach name must correspond to a security scheme which is declared in the \u003ca href=\"#securityDefinitions\"\u003eSecurity Definitions\u003c/a\u003e. If the security scheme is of type \u003ccode\u003e\"oauth2\"\u003c/code\u003e, then the value is a list of scope names required for the execution. For other security scheme types, the array MUST be empty.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv dir=\"auto\"\u003eSpecification Extensions\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-specification-extensions\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Specification Extensions\" href=\"#specification-extensions\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhile the Swagger Specification tries to accommodate most use cases, additional data can be added to extend the specification at certain points.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe extensions properties are always prefixed by \u003ccode\u003e\"x-\"\u003c/code\u003e and can have any valid JSON format value.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe extensions may or may not be supported by the available tooling, but those may be extended as well to add requested support (if tools are internal or open-sourced).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eSecurity Filtering\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-security-filtering\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Security Filtering\" href=\"#security-filtering\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSome objects in the Swagger specification may be declared and remain empty, or completely be removed, even though they are inherently the core of the API documentation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe reasoning behind it is to allow an additional layer of access control over the documentation itself. While not part of the specification itself, certain libraries may choose to allow access to parts of the documentation based on some form of authentication/authorization.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTwo examples for this:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe \u003ca href=\"#paths-object\"\u003ePaths Object\u003c/a\u003e may be empty. It may be counterintuitive, but this may tell the viewer that they got to the right place, but can't access any documentation. They'd still have access to the \u003ca href=\"#info-object\"\u003eInfo Object\u003c/a\u003e which may contain additional information regarding authentication.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe \u003ca href=\"#path-item-object\"\u003ePath Item Object\u003c/a\u003e may be empty. In this case, the viewer will be aware that the path exists, but will not be able to see any of its operations or parameters. This is different than hiding the path itself from the \u003ca href=\"#paths-object\"\u003ePaths Object\u003c/a\u003e so the user will not be aware of its existence. This allows the documentation provider a finer control over what the viewer can see.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003c/article\u003e","richTextTruncated":false,"renderedFileInfo":null,"symbols":{"timed_out":false,"not_analyzed":false,"symbols":[{"name":"OpenAPI OpenAPI Specification","## (fka Swagger RESTful API Documentation Specification)","","#### Version 2.0","","The key words \"MUST\", \"MUST NOT\", \"REQUIRED\", \"SHALL\", \"SHALL NOT\", \"SHOULD\", \"SHOULD NOT\", \"RECOMMENDED\", \"MAY\", and \"OPTIONAL\" in this document are to be interpreted as described in [RFC 2119](http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2119.txt).","","The Swagger specification is licensed under [The Apache License, Version 2.0](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html).","","## Introductions","","Swagger™  is a project used to describe and document RESTful APIs.","","The Swagger specification defines a set of files required to describe such an API. These files can then be used by the Swagger-UI project to display the API and Swagger-Codegen to generate clients in various languages. Additional utilities can also take advantage of the resulting files, such as testing tools.","","## Revision History","","Version | Date | Notes","--- | --- | ---","2.0 | 2014-09-08 | Release of Swagger 2.0","1.2 | 2014-03-14 | Initial release of the formal document.","1.1 | 2012-08-22 | Release of Swagger 1.1","1.0 | 2011-08-10 | First release of the Swagger Specification","","## Definitions","","##### Path Templating","Path templating refers to the usage of curly braces ({}) to mark a section of a URL path as replaceable using path parameters.","","##### Mime Types","Mime type definitions are spread across several resources. The mime type definitions should be in compliance with [RFC 6838](http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6838).","","Some examples of possible mime type definitions:","```","  text/plain; charset=utf-8","  application/json","  application/vnd.github+json","  application/vnd.github.v3+json","  application/vnd.github.v3.raw+json","  application/vnd.github.v3.text+json","  application/vnd.github.v3.html+json","  application/vnd.github.v3.full+json","  application/vnd.github.v3.diff","  application/vnd.github.v3.patch","```","##### HTTP Status Codes","The HTTP Status Codes are used to indicate the status of the executed operation. The available status codes are described by [RFC 7231](http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6) and in the [IANA Status Code Registry](http://www.iana.org/assignments/http-status-codes/http-status-codes.xhtml).","","## Specification","","### Format","","The files describing the RESTful API in accordance with the Swagger specification are represented as JSON objects and conform to the JSON standards. YAML, being a superset of JSON, can be used as well to","represent a Swagger specification file.","","For example, if a field is said to have an array value, the JSON array representation will be used:","","```js","{","   \"field\" : [...]","}","```","","While the API is described using JSON it does not impose a JSON input/output to the API itself.","","All field names in the specification are **case sensitive**.","","The schema exposes two types of fields. Fixed fields, which have a declared name, and Patterned fields, which declare a regex pattern for the field name. Patterned fields can have multiple occurrences as long as each has a unique name.","","### File Structure","","The Swagger representation of the API is made of a single file. However, parts of the definitions can be split into separate files, at the discretion of the user. This is applicable for `$ref` fields in the specification as follows from the [JSON Schema](http://json-schema.org) definitions.","","By convention, the Swagger specification file is named `swagger.json`.","","### Data Types","","Primitive data types in the Swagger Specification are based on the types supported by the [JSON-Schema Draft 4](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-zyp-json-schema-04#section-3.5). Models are described using the [Schema Object](#schema-object) which is a subset of JSON Schema Draft 4.","","An additional primitive data type `\"file\"` is used by the [Parameter Object](#parameter-object) and the [Response Object](#response-object) to set the parameter type or the response as being a file.","","\u003ca name=\"dataTypeFormat\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ePrimitives have an optional modifier property `format`. Swagger uses several known formats to more finely define the data type being used. However, the `format` property is an open `string`-valued property, and can have any value to support documentation needs. Formats such as `\"email\"`, `\"uuid\"`, etc., can be used even though they are not defined by this specification. Types that are not accompanied by a `format` property follow their definition from the JSON Schema (except for `file` type which is defined above). The formats defined by the Swagger Specification are:","","","Common Name | [`type`](#dataTypeType) | [`format`](#dataTypeFormat) | Comments","----------- | ------ | -------- | --------","integer | `integer` | `int32` | signed 32 bits","long | `integer` | `int64` | signed 64 bits","float | `number` | `float` | |","double | `number` | `double` | |","string | `string` | | |","byte | `string` | `byte` | base64 encoded characters","binary | `string` | `binary` | any sequence of octets","boolean | `boolean` | | |","date | `string` | `date` | As defined by `full-date` - [RFC3339](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3339#section-5.6)","dateTime | `string` | `date-time` | As defined by `date-time` - [RFC3339](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3339#section-5.6)","password | `string` | `password` | Used to hint UIs the input needs to be obscured.","","### Schema","","#### Swagger Object","","This is the root document object for the API specification. It combines what previously was the Resource Listing and API Declaration (version 1.2 and earlier) together into one document.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"swaggerSwagger\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eswagger | `string` | **Required.** Specifies the Swagger Specification version being used. It can be used by the Swagger UI and other clients to interpret the API listing. The value MUST be `\"2.0\"`.","\u003ca name=\"swaggerInfo\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003einfo | [Info Object](#info-object) | **Required.** Provides metadata about the API. The metadata can be used by the clients if needed.","\u003ca name=\"swaggerHost\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ehost | `string` | The host (name or ip) serving the API. This MUST be the host only and does not include the scheme nor sub-paths. It MAY include a port. If the `host` is not included, the host serving the documentation is to be used (including the port). The `host` does not support [path templating](#path-templating).","\u003ca name=\"swaggerBasePath\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ebasePath | `string` | The base path on which the API is served, which is relative to the [`host`](#swaggerHost). If it is not included, the API is served directly under the `host`. The value MUST start with a leading slash (`/`). The `basePath` does not support [path templating](#path-templating).","\u003ca name=\"swaggerSchemes\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eschemes | [`string`] | The transfer protocol of the API. Values MUST be from the list: `\"http\"`, `\"https\"`, `\"ws\"`, `\"wss\"`. If the `schemes` is not included, the default scheme to be used is the one used to access the Swagger definition itself.","\u003ca name=\"swaggerConsumes\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003econsumes | [`string`] | A list of MIME types the APIs can consume. This is global to all APIs but can be overridden on specific API calls. Value MUST be as described under [Mime Types](#mime-types).","\u003ca name=\"swaggerProduces\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eproduces | [`string`] | A list of MIME types the APIs can produce. This is global to all APIs but can be overridden on specific API calls. Value MUST be as described under [Mime Types](#mime-types).","\u003ca name=\"swaggerPaths\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003epaths | [Paths Object](#paths-object) | **Required.** The available paths and operations for the API.","\u003ca name=\"swaggerDefinitions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edefinitions | [Definitions Object](#definitions-object) | An object to hold data types produced and consumed by operations.","\u003ca name=\"swaggerParameters\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eparameters | [Parameters Definitions Object](#parameters-definitions-object) | An object to hold parameters that can be used across operations. This property *does not* define global parameters for all operations.","\u003ca name=\"swaggerResponses\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eresponses | [Responses Definitions Object](#responses-definitions-object) | An object to hold responses that can be used across operations. This property *does not* define global responses for all operations.","\u003ca name=\"swaggerSecurityDefinitions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003esecurityDefinitions | [Security Definitions Object](#security-definitions-object) | Security scheme definitions that can be used across the specification.","\u003ca name=\"swaggerSecurity\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003esecurity | [[Security Requirement Object](#security-requirement-object)] | A declaration of which security schemes are applied for the API as a whole. The list of values describes alternative security schemes that can be used (that is, there is a logical OR between the security requirements). Individual operations can override this definition.","\u003ca name=\"swaggerTags\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etags | [[Tag Object](#tag-object)] | A list of tags used by the specification with additional metadata. The order of the tags can be used to reflect on their order by the parsing tools. Not all tags that are used by the [Operation Object](#operation-object) must be declared. The tags that are not declared may be organized randomly or based on the tools' logic. Each tag name in the list MUST be unique.","\u003ca name=\"swaggerExternalDocs\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexternalDocs | [External Documentation Object](#external-documentation-object) | Additional external documentation.","","##### Patterned Objects","","Field Pattern | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"swaggerExtensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x- | Any | Allows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with `x-`, for example, `x-internal-id`. The value can be `null`, a primitive, an array or an object. See [Vendor Extensions](#specification-extensions) for further details.","","#### Info Object","","The object provides metadata about the API. The metadata can be used by the clients if needed, and can be presented in the Swagger-UI for convenience.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"infoTitle\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etitle | `string` | **Required.** The title of the application.","\u003ca name=\"infoDescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription | `string` | A short description of the application. [GFM syntax](https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/#GitHub-flavored-markdown) can be used for rich text representation.","\u003ca name=\"infoTermsOfService\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etermsOfService | `string` | The Terms of Service for the API.","\u003ca name=\"infoContact\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003econtact | [Contact Object](#contact-object) | The contact information for the exposed API.","\u003ca name=\"infoLicense\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003elicense | [License Object](#license-object) | The license information for the exposed API.","\u003ca name=\"infoVersion\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eversion | `string` | **Required** Provides the version of the application API (not to be confused with the specification version).","","##### Patterned Objects","","Field Pattern | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"infoExtensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x- | Any | Allows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with `x-`, for example, `x-internal-id`. The value can be `null`, a primitive, an array or an object. See [Vendor Extensions](#specification-extensions) for further details.","","##### Info Object Example:","","```js","{","  \"title\": \"Swagger Sample App\",","  \"description\": \"This is a sample server Petstore server.\",","  \"termsOfService\": \"http://swagger.io/terms/\",","  \"contact\": {","    \"name\": \"API Support\",","    \"url\": \"http://www.swagger.io/support\",","    \"email\": \"support@swagger.io\"","  },","  \"license\": {","    \"name\": \"Apache 2.0\",","    \"url\": \"http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html\"","  },","  \"version\": \"1.0.1\"","}","```","","```yaml","title: Swagger Sample App","description: This is a sample server Petstore server.","termsOfService: http://swagger.io/terms/","contact:","  name: API Support","  url: http://www.swagger.io/support","  email: support@swagger.io","license:","  name: Apache 2.0","  url: http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html","version: 1.0.1","```","","#### Contact Object","","Contact information for the exposed API.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"contactName\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ename | `string` | The identifying name of the contact person/organization.","\u003ca name=\"contactUrl\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eurl | `string` | The URL pointing to the contact information. MUST be in the format of a URL.","\u003ca name=\"contactEmail\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eemail | `string` | The email address of the contact person/organization. MUST be in the format of an email address.","","##### Patterned Objects","","Field Pattern | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"contactExtensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x- | Any | Allows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with `x-`, for example, `x-internal-id`. The value can be `null`, a primitive, an array or an object. See [Vendor Extensions](#specification-extensions) for further details.","","##### Contact Object Example:","","```js","{","  \"name\": \"API Support\",","  \"url\": \"http://www.swagger.io/support\",","  \"email\": \"support@swagger.io\"","}","```","","```yaml","name: API Support","url: http://www.swagger.io/support","email: support@swagger.io","```","","#### License Object","","License information for the exposed API.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"licenseName\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ename | `string` | **Required.** The license name used for the API.","\u003ca name=\"licenseUrl\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eurl | `string` | A URL to the license used for the API. MUST be in the format of a URL.","","##### Patterned Objects","","Field Pattern | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"licenseExtensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x- | Any | Allows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with `x-`, for example, `x-internal-id`. The value can be `null`, a primitive, an array or an object. See [Vendor Extensions](#specification-extensions) for further details.","","##### License Object Example:","","```js","{","  \"name\": \"Apache 2.0\",","  \"url\": \"http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html\"","}","```","","```yaml","name: Apache 2.0","url: http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html","```","","#### Paths Object","","Holds the relative paths to the individual endpoints. The path is appended to the [`basePath`](#swaggerBasePath) in order to construct the full URL.","The Paths may be empty, due to [ACL constraints](#security-filtering).","","##### Patterned Fields","","Field Pattern | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"pathsPath\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e/{path} | [Path Item Object](#path-item-object) | A relative path to an individual endpoint. The field name MUST begin with a slash. The path is appended to the [`basePath`](#swaggerBasePath) in order to construct the full URL. [Path templating](#path-templating) is allowed.","\u003ca name=\"pathsExtensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x- | Any | Allows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with `x-`, for example, `x-internal-id`. The value can be `null`, a primitive, an array or an object. See [Vendor Extensions](#specification-extensions) for further details.","","##### Paths Object Example","","```js","{","  \"/pets\": {","    \"get\": {","      \"description\": \"Returns all pets from the system that the user has access to\",","      \"produces\": [","        \"application/json\"","      ],","      \"responses\": {","        \"200\": {","          \"description\": \"A list of pets.\",","          \"schema\": {","            \"type\": \"array\",","            \"items\": {","              \"$ref\": \"#/definitions/pet\"","            }","          }","        }","      }","    }","  }","}","```","","```yaml","/pets:","  get:","    description: Returns all pets from the system that the user has access to","    produces:","    - application/json","    responses:","      '200':","        description: A list of pets.","        schema:","          type: array","          items:","            $ref: '#/definitions/pet'","```","","#### Path Item Object","","Describes the operations available on a single path.","A Path Item may be empty, due to [ACL constraints](#security-filtering). The path itself is still exposed to the documentation viewer but they will not know which operations and parameters are available.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"pathItemRef\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e$ref | `string` | Allows for an external definition of this path item. The referenced structure MUST be in the format of a [Path Item Object](#path-item-object). If there are conflicts between the referenced definition and this Path Item's definition, the behavior is *undefined*.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemGet\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eget | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a GET operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemPut\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eput | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a PUT operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemPost\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003epost | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a POST operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemDelete\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edelete | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a DELETE operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemOptions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eoptions | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a OPTIONS operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemHead\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ehead | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a HEAD operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemPatch\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003epatch | [Operation Object](#operation-object) | A definition of a PATCH operation on this path.","\u003ca name=\"pathItemParameters\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eparameters | [[Parameter Object](#parameter-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | A list of parameters that are applicable for all the operations described under this path. These parameters can be overridden at the operation level, but cannot be removed there. The list MUST NOT include duplicated parameters. A unique parameter is defined by a combination of a [name](#parameterName) and [location](#parameterIn). The list can use the [Reference Object](#reference-object) to link to parameters that are defined at the [Swagger Object's parameters](#swaggerParameters). There can be one \"body\" parameter at most.","","##### Patterned Fields","","Field Pattern | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"pathItemExtensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x- | Any | Allows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with `x-`, for example, `x-internal-id`. The value can be `null`, a primitive, an array or an object. See [Vendor Extensions](#specification-extensions) for further details.","","##### Path Item Object Example","","```js","{","  \"get\": {","    \"description\": \"Returns pets based on ID\",","    \"summary\": \"Find pets by ID\",","    \"operationId\": \"getPetsById\",","    \"produces\": [","      \"application/json\",","      \"text/html\"","    ],","    \"responses\": {","      \"200\": {","        \"description\": \"pet response\",","        \"schema\": {","          \"type\": \"array\",","          \"items\": {","            \"$ref\": \"#/definitions/Pet\"","          }","        }","      },","      \"default\": {","        \"description\": \"error payload\",","        \"schema\": {","          \"$ref\": \"#/definitions/ErrorModel\"","        }","      }","    }","  },","  \"parameters\": [","    {","      \"name\": \"id\",","      \"in\": \"path\",","      \"description\": \"ID of pet to use\",","      \"required\": true,","      \"type\": \"array\",","      \"items\": {","        \"type\": \"string\"","      },","      \"collectionFormat\": \"csv\"","    }","  ]","}","```","","```yaml","get:","  description: Returns pets based on ID","  summary: Find pets by ID","  operationId: getPetsById","  produces:","  - application/json","  - text/html","  responses:","    '200':","      description: pet response","      schema:","        type: array","        items:","          $ref: '#/definitions/Pet'","    default:","      description: error payload","      schema:","        $ref: '#/definitions/ErrorModel'","parameters:","- name: id","  in: path","  description: ID of pet to use","  required: true","  type: array","  items:","    type: string","  collectionFormat: csv","```","","#### Operation Object","","Describes a single API operation on a path.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"operationTags\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etags | [`string`] | A list of tags for API documentation control. Tags can be used for logical grouping of operations by resources or any other qualifier.","\u003ca name=\"operationSummary\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003esummary | `string` | A short summary of what the operation does. For maximum readability in the swagger-ui, this field SHOULD be less than 120 characters.","\u003ca name=\"operationDescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription | `string` | A verbose explanation of the operation behavior. [GFM syntax](https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/#GitHub-flavored-markdown) can be used for rich text representation.","\u003ca name=\"operationExternalDocs\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexternalDocs | [External Documentation Object](#external-documentation-object) | Additional external documentation for this operation.","\u003ca name=\"operationId\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eoperationId | `string` | Unique string used to identify the operation. The id MUST be unique among all operations described in the API. Tools and libraries MAY use the operationId to uniquely identify an operation, therefore, it is recommended to follow common programming naming conventions.","\u003ca name=\"operationConsumes\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003econsumes | [`string`] | A list of MIME types the operation can consume. This overrides the [`consumes`](#swaggerConsumes) definition at the Swagger Object. An empty value MAY be used to clear the global definition. Value MUST be as described under [Mime Types](#mime-types).","\u003ca name=\"operationProduces\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eproduces | [`string`] | A list of MIME types the operation can produce. This overrides the [`produces`](#swaggerProduces) definition at the Swagger Object. An empty value MAY be used to clear the global definition. Value MUST be as described under [Mime Types](#mime-types).","\u003ca name=\"operationParameters\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eparameters | [[Parameter Object](#parameter-object) \\| [Reference Object](#reference-object)] | A list of parameters that are applicable for this operation. If a parameter is already defined at the [Path Item](#pathItemParameters), the new definition will override it, but can never remove it. The list MUST NOT include duplicated parameters. A unique parameter is defined by a combination of a [name](#parameterName) and [location](#parameterIn). The list can use the [Reference Object](#reference-object) to link to parameters that are defined at the [Swagger Object's parameters](#swaggerParameters). There can be one \"body\" parameter at most.","\u003ca name=\"operationResponses\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eresponses | [Responses Object](#responses-object) | **Required.** The list of possible responses as they are returned from executing this operation.","\u003ca name=\"operationSchemes\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eschemes | [`string`] | The transfer protocol for the operation. Values MUST be from the list: `\"http\"`, `\"https\"`, `\"ws\"`, `\"wss\"`. The value overrides the Swagger Object [`schemes`](#swaggerSchemes) definition.","\u003ca name=\"operationDeprecated\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edeprecated | `boolean` | Declares this operation to be deprecated. Usage of the declared operation should be refrained. Default value is `false`.","\u003ca name=\"operationSecurity\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003esecurity | [[Security Requirement Object](#security-requirement-object)] | A declaration of which security schemes are applied for this operation. The list of values describes alternative security schemes that can be used (that is, there is a logical OR between the security requirements). This definition overrides any declared top-level [`security`](#swaggerSecurity). To remove a top-level security declaration, an empty array can be used.","","##### Patterned Objects","","Field Pattern | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"operationExtensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x- | Any | Allows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with `x-`, for example, `x-internal-id`. The value can be `null`, a primitive, an array or an object. See [Vendor Extensions](#specification-extensions) for further details.","","##### Operation Object Example","","```js","{","  \"tags\": [","    \"pet\"","  ],","  \"summary\": \"Updates a pet in the store with form data\",","  \"description\": \"\",","  \"operationId\": \"updatePetWithForm\",","  \"consumes\": [","    \"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\"","  ],","  \"produces\": [","    \"application/json\",","    \"application/xml\"","  ],","  \"parameters\": [","    {","      \"name\": \"petId\",","      \"in\": \"path\",","      \"description\": \"ID of pet that needs to be updated\",","      \"required\": true,","      \"type\": \"string\"","    },","    {","      \"name\": \"name\",","      \"in\": \"formData\",","      \"description\": \"Updated name of the pet\",","      \"required\": false,","      \"type\": \"string\"","    },","    {","      \"name\": \"status\",","      \"in\": \"formData\",","      \"description\": \"Updated status of the pet\",","      \"required\": false,","      \"type\": \"string\"","    }","  ],","  \"responses\": {","    \"200\": {","      \"description\": \"Pet updated.\"","    },","    \"405\": {","      \"description\": \"Invalid input\"","    }","  },","  \"security\": [","    {","      \"petstore_auth\": [","        \"write:pets\",","        \"read:pets\"","      ]","    }","  ]","}","```","","```yaml","tags:","- pet","summary: Updates a pet in the store with form data","description: \"\"","operationId: updatePetWithForm","consumes:","- application/x-www-form-urlencoded","produces:","- application/json","- application/xml","parameters:","- name: petId","  in: path","  description: ID of pet that needs to be updated","  required: true","  type: string","- name: name","  in: formData","  description: Updated name of the pet","  required: false","  type: string","- name: status","  in: formData","  description: Updated status of the pet","  required: false","  type: string","responses:","  '200':","    description: Pet updated.","  '405':","    description: Invalid input","security:","- petstore_auth:","  - write:pets","  - read:pets","```","","","#### External Documentation Object","","Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.","","##### Fixed Fields","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"externalDocDescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription | `string` | A short description of the target documentation. [GFM syntax](https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/#GitHub-flavored-markdown) can be used for rich text representation.","\u003ca name=\"externalDocUrl\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eurl | `string` | **Required.** The URL for the target documentation. Value MUST be in the format of a URL.","","##### Patterned Objects","","Field Pattern | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"externalDocExtensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x- | Any | Allows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with `x-`, for example, `x-internal-id`. The value can be `null`, a primitive, an array or an object. See [Vendor Extensions](#specification-extensions) for further details.","","##### External Documentation Object Example","","```js","{","  \"description\": \"Find more info here\",","  \"url\": \"https://swagger.io\"","}","```","","```yaml","description: Find more info here","url: https://swagger.io","```","","#### Parameter Object","","Describes a single operation parameter.","","A unique parameter is defined by a combination of a [name](#parameterName) and [location](#parameterIn).","","There are five possible parameter types.","* Path - Used together with [Path Templating](#path-templating), where the parameter value is actually part of the operation's URL. This does not include the host or base path of the API. For example, in `/items/{itemId}`, the path parameter is `itemId`.","* Query - Parameters that are appended to the URL. For example, in `/items?id=###`, the query parameter is `id`.","* Header - Custom headers that are expected as part of the request.","* Body - The payload that's appended to the HTTP request. Since there can only be one payload, there can only be *one* body parameter. The name of the body parameter has no effect on the parameter itself and is used for documentation purposes only. Since Form parameters are also in the payload, body and form parameters cannot exist together for the same operation.","* Form - Used to describe the payload of an HTTP request when either `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`, `multipart/form-data` or both are used as the content type of the request (in Swagger's definition, the [`consumes`](#operationConsumes) property of an operation). This is the only parameter type that can be used to send files, thus supporting the `file` type. Since form parameters are sent in the payload, they cannot be declared together with a body parameter for the same operation. Form parameters have a different format based on the content-type used (for further details, consult http://www.w3.org/TR/html401/interact/forms.html#h-17.13.4):","  * `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` - Similar to the format of Query parameters but as a payload. For example, `foo=1\u0026bar=swagger` - both `foo` and `bar` are form parameters. This is normally used for simple parameters that are being transferred.","  * `multipart/form-data` - each parameter takes a section in the payload with an internal header. For example, for the header `Content-Disposition: form-data; name=\"submit-name\"` the name of the parameter is `submit-name`. This type of form parameters is more commonly used for file transfers.","","##### Fixed Fields","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"parameterName\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ename | `string` | **Required.** The name of the parameter. Parameter names are *case sensitive*. \u003cul\u003e\u003cli\u003eIf [`in`](#parameterIn) is `\"path\"`, the `name` field MUST correspond to the associated path segment from the [path](#pathsPath) field in the [Paths Object](#paths-object). See [Path Templating](#path-templating) for further information.\u003cli\u003eFor all other cases, the `name` corresponds to the parameter name used based on the [`in`](#parameterIn) property.\u003c/ul\u003e","\u003ca name=\"parameterIn\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ein | `string` | **Required.** The location of the parameter. Possible values are \"query\", \"header\", \"path\", \"formData\" or \"body\".","\u003ca name=\"parameterDescription\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edescription | `string` | A brief description of the parameter. This could contain examples of use.  [GFM syntax](https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/#GitHub-flavored-markdown) can be used for rich text representation.","\u003ca name=\"parameterRequired\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003erequired | `boolean` | Determines whether this parameter is mandatory. If the parameter is [`in`](#parameterIn) \"path\", this property is **required** and its value MUST be `true`. Otherwise, the property MAY be included and its default value is `false`.","","If [`in`](#parameterIn) is `\"body\"`:","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"parameterSchema\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eschema | [Schema Object](#schema-object) | **Required.** The schema defining the type used for the body parameter.","","If [`in`](#parameterIn) is any value other than `\"body\"`:","","Field Name | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"parameterType\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003etype | `string` | **Required.** The type of the parameter. Since the parameter is not located at the request body, it is limited to simple types (that is, not an object). The value MUST be one of `\"string\"`, `\"number\"`, `\"integer\"`, `\"boolean\"`, `\"array\"` or `\"file\"`. If `type` is `\"file\"`, the [`consumes`](#operationConsumes) MUST be either `\"multipart/form-data\"`, `\" application/x-www-form-urlencoded\"` or both and the parameter MUST be [`in`](#parameterIn) `\"formData\"`.","\u003ca name=\"parameterFormat\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eformat | `string` | The extending format for the previously mentioned [`type`](#parameterType). See [Data Type Formats](#dataTypeFormat) for further details.","\u003ca name=\"parameterAllowEmptyValue\"/\u003eallowEmptyValue | `boolean` | Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters. This is valid only for either `query` or `formData` parameters and allows you to send a parameter with a name only or  an empty value. Default value is `false`.","\u003ca name=\"parameterItems\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eitems | [Items Object](#items-object) | **Required if [`type`](#parameterType) is \"array\".** Describes the type of items in the array.","\u003ca name=\"parameterCollectionFormat\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003ecollectionFormat | `string` | Determines the format of the array if type array is used. Possible values are: \u003cul\u003e\u003cli\u003e`csv` - comma separated values `foo,bar`. \u003cli\u003e`ssv` - space separated values `foo bar`. \u003cli\u003e`tsv` - tab separated values `foo\\tbar`. \u003cli\u003e`pipes` - pipe separated values \u003ccode\u003efoo\u0026#124;bar\u003c/code\u003e. \u003cli\u003e`multi` - corresponds to multiple parameter instances instead of multiple values for a single instance `foo=bar\u0026foo=baz`. This is valid only for parameters [`in`](#parameterIn) \"query\" or \"formData\". \u003c/ul\u003e Default value is `csv`.","\u003ca name=\"parameterDefault\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003edefault | * | Declares the value of the parameter that the server will use if none is provided, for example a \"count\" to control the number of results per page might default to 100 if not supplied by the client in the request. (Note: \"default\" has no meaning for required parameters.)  See https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-6.2. Unlike JSON Schema this value MUST conform to the defined [`type`](#parameterType) for this parameter.","\u003ca name=\"parameterMaximum\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003emaximum | `number` | See https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.2.","\u003ca name=\"parameterExclusiveMaximum\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexclusiveMaximum | `boolean` | See https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.2.","\u003ca name=\"parameterMinimum\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eminimum | `number` | See https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.3.","\u003ca name=\"parameterExclusiveMinimum\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eexclusiveMinimum | `boolean` | See https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.3.","\u003ca name=\"parameterMaxLength\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003emaxLength | `integer` | See https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.2.1.","\u003ca name=\"parameterMinLength\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eminLength | `integer` | See https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.2.2.","\u003ca name=\"parameterPattern\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003epattern | `string` | See https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.2.3.","\u003ca name=\"parameterMaxItems\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003emaxItems | `integer` | See https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.3.2.","\u003ca name=\"parameterMinItems\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eminItems | `integer` | See https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.3.3.","\u003ca name=\"parameterUniqueItems\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003euniqueItems | `boolean` | See https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.3.4.","\u003ca name=\"parameterEnum\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003eenum | [*] | See https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.5.1.","\u003ca name=\"parameterMultipleOf\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003emultipleOf | `number` | See https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.1.1.","","","##### Patterned Fields","Field Pattern | Type | Description","---|:---:|---","\u003ca name=\"parameterExtensions\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e^x- | Any | Allows extensions to the Swagger Schema. The field name MUST begin with `x-`, for example, `x-internal-id`. The value can be `null`, a primitive, an array or an object. See [Vendor Extensions](#specification-extensions) for further details.","","","##### Parameter Object Examples","","###### Body Parameters","","A body parameter with a referenced schema definition (normally for a model definition):","```js","{","  \"name\": \"user\",","  \"in\": \"body\",","  \"description\": \"user to add to the system\",","  \"required\": true,","  \"schema\": {","    \"$ref\": \"#/definitions/User\"","  }","}","```","","```yaml","name: user","in: body","description: user to add to the system","required: true","schema:","  $ref: '#/definitions/User'","```","","A body parameter that is an array of string values:","```js","{","  \"name\": \"user\",","  \"in\": \"body\",","  \"description\": \"user to add to the system\",","  \"required\": true,","  \"schema\": {","    \"type\": \"array\",","    \"items\": {","      \"type\": \"string\"","    }","  }","}","```","","```yaml","name: user","in: body","description: user to add to the system","required: true","schema:","  type: array","  items:","    type: string","```","","###### Other Parameters","","A header parameter with an array of 64 bit integer numbers:","","```js","{","  \"name\": \"token\",","  \"in\": \"header\",","  \"description\": \"token to be passed as a header\",","  \"required\": true,","  \"type\": \"array\",","  \"items\": {","    \"type\": \"integer\",","    \"format\": \"int64\"","  },","  \"collectionFormat\": \"csv\"","}","```","","```yaml","name: token","in: header","description: token to be passed as a header","required: true","type:
